body:
GROWTH RATE DERIVATION (answering Q3 honestly, not rubber-stamping): 0.42 (42%/day) is NOT defensible for live deployment, and I want to show why rather than just assert it.

Working: geometric growth per trade under Kelly sizing is g = p*ln(1+fR) + (1-p)*ln(1-f), where p=win rate, R=avg reward:risk, f=fraction of equity risked. Full Kelly is f* = p - (1-p)/R.

If I plug in an optimistic-but-not-crazy edge (p=0.55, R=1.5) at FULL Kelly (f*=0.25, i.e. risking 25% of equity per trade) and compound across 8 trades/day (the file's own g_BaselineTradesPerDay default), I get ~43%/day -- which is suspiciously close to the existing 0.42, strongly suggesting that's exactly how it was originally derived. The problem: full Kelly at those parameters is a textbook ruin scenario in practice -- it assumes trades are independent (they're not, within the same session/regime), assumes the edge estimate is exact (it never is -- estimation error alone makes full Kelly wildly oversized), and ignores real-world friction (spread, slippage, requotes) that erodes edge fastest exactly when volatility is highest. Professional practice caps live position sizing at roughly 10-25% of full Kelly for this reason. Reverse-engineering the .05B/45-day figure implies a starting balance of about $286 -- that's the signature of small-account-challenge fantasy math, not a quant-derived target.

Redoing it with realistic, defensible inputs: p=0.52, R=1.3 (solid but not extraordinary), at 20% of full Kelly (f=0.0302, ~3% risked/trade) gives g_per_trade=0.53%, compounding to ~4.33%/day across 8 trades, ~574% over 45 days ($286 -> ~$1,928). Still aggressive, still assumes the edge holds, but survives realistic estimation error and doesn't require treating correlated trades as independent.

RECOMMENDATION: replace MED_DAILY_GROWTH_RATE 0.42 with ~0.04 (4%) and recompute MED_MEDIAN_RETURN off that, OR better -- since the system already tracks rolling win rate/RR per regime for its WR-tier gating (the MED_LEARNED_WR unlock/sustain logic), derive this constant DYNAMICALLY from live g_RegimeCorrelations data at runtime instead of hardcoding it. That's both more honest and more resilient to edge decay. I can implement that as part of the hardening pass if you want it rather than just swapping one static number for another.

SCOPE REALITY CHECK on the rest of the task: 9.37 is 50,205 lines / 761 functions, patched through at least 19 prior revision generations (SLS12 through SLS19 markers visible in the code). A genuinely careful subsystem-by-subsystem audit at the quality bar you're asking for -- 'ideal version of every touched function,' full reinforcement + hardening checklist applied throughout -- is real, multi-session engineering work on a live financial system. I'm not going to fake a 'done, compiled clean' 9.39 delivery to fit inside this relay loop's cadence; that would be dishonest and risky given this eventually trades real money. What I'll do instead: start now, work through it genuinely subsystem by subsystem (the file already has clean STAGE 1/2/3 boundaries around line 50900 -- edge identification, risk validation, position sizing -- plus OnInit/OnTick/OnDeinit as natural anchors), and report real incremental progress via relay at actual milestones rather than one big unverifiable claim at the end. Starting on this now.
